data.title: ArcelorMittal posts Q2 2026 analyst consensus, distances itself from estimates
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

ArcelorMittal ( (NL:MT) ) has shared an update.

ArcelorMittal has published second-quarter 2026 sell-side analyst consensus figures compiled by independent data provider Visible Alpha, drawing on estimates from 13 of roughly 15 brokers that cover the stock. The aggregated consensus points to expected EBITDA of $2.04 billion, net income of $802 million and earnings per share of $1.06 for the quarter.

The company stresses that these forecasts reflect external analysts’ views rather than internal guidance, noting it neither collects nor validates the underlying data. By distancing itself from the consensus methodology and accuracy, ArcelorMittal signals that investors should treat the figures as an outside snapshot of market expectations rather than an official outlook on its forthcoming results.

The most recent analyst rating on (NL:MT) stock is a Hold
with a EUR57.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ArcelorMittal stock,
see the NL:MT Stock Forecast page.

More about ArcelorMittal

ArcelorMittal is one of the world’s leading integrated steel and mining companies, with operations in 60 countries and primary steelmaking in 14. It is the largest steel producer in Europe, a major player in the Americas, and is expanding in Asia via its AM/NS India joint venture, supplying automotive, engineering, construction and machinery customers while pursuing lower-carbon, higher-performance steels.

Average Trading Volume: 2,138,171

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: €43.32B

data.title: Liberty Global Posts Solid Q2 Results, Expands Fiber Footprint
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Liberty Global A ( (LBTYA) ) is now available.

Liberty Global reported its second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, highlighting solid operational performance across its European telecom businesses, including broadband subscriber gains at VodafoneZiggo and Telenet and improved trading at Virgin Media O2, alongside continued fiber network expansion to 9 million premises in the UK and sustained mobile and broadband growth in Ireland. The company also advanced preparations for a planned Ziggo Group spin-off in 2027, exited its remaining EdgeConneX stake for $604 million, lifted year-to-date asset monetizations to about $1.2 billion and raised its year-end corporate cash target to roughly $2.0 billion, underscoring a strategy of asset rotation, balance-sheet strengthening and shareholder value focus.

The most recent analyst rating on (LBTYA) stock is a Hold
with a $12.10 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Liberty Global A stock,
see the LBTYA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LBTYA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LBTYA is a Neutral.

LBTYA scores around the middle primarily because financial performance is mixed—stable revenue and positive (but weakened) free cash flow are outweighed by large recent net losses and deteriorating returns/leverage. Technicals further pressure the score due to a clear downtrend across moving averages and negative MACD. Offsetting these risks, the latest earnings call was moderately positive with reaffirmed 2026 guidance, improving broadband trends, and progress on strategic value-unlock initiatives, while valuation remains hard to assess given negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on LBTYA stock,
click here.

More about Liberty Global A

Liberty Global Ltd. is an international telecommunications and media company with operations across Europe, including the Benelux region, the UK and Ireland. Through platforms such as VodafoneZiggo, Telenet, Virgin Media O2 and Virgin Media Ireland, it provides broadband, mobile and converged telecom services, and is increasingly focused on full fiber networks and infrastructure-driven growth.

Average Trading Volume: 1,888,511

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $3.5B

Learn more about LBTYA stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: HCA Healthcare Posts Solid Q2 2026 Results, Trims Outlook
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

HCA Healthcare ( (HCA) ) has provided an update.

HCA Healthcare reported its second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, 2026, posting an 8.7% year-on-year rise in revenue to $20.23 billion and a 2.8% increase in net income to $1.70 billion, with diluted EPS up 11.6% to $7.62 and adjusted EBITDA up 4.6% to $4.03 billion. Same-facility admissions rose 2.5% and ER visits 3.6%, while inpatient and outpatient surgeries declined, and the company also disclosed that first-half 2026 revenue reached $39.34 billion with net income of $3.32 billion.

The quarter was shaped by a negative payer mix shift from higher uninsured volumes tied to health insurance exchange coverage losses, which HCA estimated reduced pre-tax income by about $400 million, partially offset by roughly $400 million of incremental net benefit from Medicaid Supplemental Payment Programs, largely from Florida. Cash flow from operations fell sharply to $2.34 billion from $4.21 billion a year earlier, as HCA spent $1.23 billion on capital expenditures, repurchased 4.75 million shares for $2.06 billion, and maintained sizeable debt of $49.72 billion, while its board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.78 per share and the company trimmed full-year 2026 guidance to reflect greater exchange-related headwinds and higher expected Medicaid support.

The most recent analyst rating on (HCA) stock is a Hold
with a $369.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on HCA Healthcare stock,
see the HCA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HCA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HCA is a Neutral.

The score is anchored by strong earnings and cash generation but is pulled down by elevated balance-sheet risk (negative equity) and weak technical trend (price below all key moving averages with negative momentum). Valuation is a supportive factor (P/E 12.43), while recent updates and the earnings call show resilience but highlight meaningful payer-mix/exchange headwinds and slightly lower 2026 guidance.

To see Spark’s full report on HCA stock,
click here.

More about HCA Healthcare

HCA Healthcare, Inc. is a large U.S. hospital operator, running acute care hospitals and related healthcare facilities with a focus on inpatient, outpatient and emergency services. The company generates revenue primarily from patient admissions and procedures, and its performance is closely tied to payer mix dynamics, government programs and broader health policy developments affecting reimbursement.

Average Trading Volume: 1,621,059

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $82.5B

For an in-depth examination of HCA stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Fiinu shareholders back most AGM resolutions but block non-pre-emptive share issuance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Immedia ( (GB:BANK) ) has provided an announcement.

Fiinu Plc reported that shareholders approved 12 of 13 resolutions at its latest Annual General Meeting, including the adoption of 2025 accounts, the directors’ remuneration report and policy, the appointment and remuneration of the auditor, and the re-election of most board members. Authority was also granted for directors to allot equity securities and to make market purchases of the company’s own shares, indicating continued support for existing governance and capital management structures.

However, shareholders narrowly declined to grant the board authority to issue equity for cash on a non-pre-emptive basis, as the resolution failed to reach the required 75% threshold despite a simple majority in favour. This outcome signals some investor caution over potential dilution and limits the company’s flexibility to raise capital quickly without offering existing shareholders pre-emptive rights, which may influence Fiinu’s future funding strategies and balance between growth ambitions and shareholder protections.

More about Immedia

Fiinu Plc is a UK-based fintech group founded in 2017 that has developed the Plugin Overdraft, an unbundled overdraft product allowing customers to secure overdraft facilities without changing their existing bank. Its Bank Independent Overdraft platform is bank-agnostic and leverages Open Banking to attach to customers’ existing accounts, aiming to enhance competition and innovation in the UK banking market.

Fiinu’s technology integrates with multiple banks via Open Banking APIs, enabling overdraft services to be provided across different institutions without requiring account migration. The company positions itself as an infrastructure provider within the UK financial ecosystem, targeting consumers who seek flexible overdraft solutions and banks that wish to augment their offerings without building equivalent technology in-house.

Average Trading Volume: 347,391

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £14.91M

data.title: China Agri-Products Exchange, Wang On Group Seal RMB64 Million Sale-Leaseback with Haier
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited ( (HK:0149) ) has issued an announcement.

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited and its parent Wang On Group Limited have entered into a sale and leaseback arrangement through their subsidiaries Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin with Haier as lessor, involving assets sold for RMB64 million, or about HK$73.81 million. The price was set after arm’s-length negotiations, referencing asset costs and an acceptable loan-to-value ratio, and will be paid within 10 business days once specified conditions, including completion of equity pledge registrations by Henan Gangan and Crown Fortress in favour of Haier, are satisfied.

The transaction qualifies as a discloseable transaction for both companies under Hong Kong Listing Rules, as the highest applicable percentage ratio exceeds 5% but is below 25%. This financing move underscores the group’s use of asset-backed structures to unlock liquidity while retaining operational use of the assets, which may bolster its capital flexibility and support ongoing operations without triggering more stringent shareholder approval requirements.

More about China Agri-Products Exchange Limited

China Agri-Products Exchange Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates within the agricultural products and related exchange sector, focusing on the development and operation of agri-product trading and logistics platforms in mainland China. It is affiliated with Wang On Group Limited, which has diversified interests and uses project companies such as Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin to hold and manage specific agricultural market assets.

YTD Price Performance: -10.67%

Average Trading Volume: 3,264,936

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$637M

Jamna Auto Industries Limited ( (IN:JAMNAAUTO) ) just unveiled an update.

Jamna Auto Industries has released an investor presentation detailing its proposed acquisition of Owen Springs Limited, a company incorporated in England and Wales. The move is positioned as a strategic entry into the UK leaf spring aftermarket, signaling the company’s intent to expand its geographic footprint and strengthen its presence in international replacement parts markets.

By targeting a specialist UK player, Jamna Auto aims to enhance its access to European customers and leverage synergies in product offerings and distribution. The proposed deal underscores the company’s broader growth strategy in suspension components, with potential implications for its competitive positioning and scale in the global leaf spring aftermarket.

More about Jamna Auto Industries Limited

Jamna Auto Industries Limited is an Indian manufacturer in the automotive components sector, known for producing leaf springs and related suspension products for commercial vehicles and aftermarket customers. The company operates multiple manufacturing and corporate locations across India and focuses on both domestic and international markets for its suspension solutions.

data.title: China Power flags up to 57% profit drop on weaker wind and solar, softer coal demand
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has issued an announcement.

China Power International Development warned that profit attributable to ordinary shareholders for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB1.1 billion to RMB1.4 billion, a drop of roughly 45% to 57% from a year earlier. The company cited weaker wind and solar resources, heightened tariff volatility in China’s competitive power market and lower coal-fired electricity sales as key drivers of the earnings decline.

Management said strong rainfall boosted hydropower output at subsidiary SPIC Hydropower, delivering a significant profit increase that partially offset the shortfall in other segments. The figures are based on unaudited internal accounts, and the company plans to release its formal interim results in late August 2026, while advising shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its shares.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold
with a HK$3.30 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock,
see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong–incorporated power producer focused on generating electricity from a diversified mix of wind, solar, hydropower and coal-fired plants across mainland China. The group, listed in Hong Kong, also controls Shanghai-listed SPIC Hydropower, highlighting its strategic emphasis on renewable and low-carbon energy assets within China’s evolving power market.

Average Trading Volume: 30,768,659

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$35.75B

Lohalma S.r.l., a legal entity closely associated with Tamburi Investment Partners executive director and general manager Claudio Berretti, has purchased 1,000 ordinary shares of the company. data.title: TSMC Details June 2026 Insider Share Changes and NT$11.6 Billion Fixed-Income Investments
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

TSMC ( (TSM) ) has provided an announcement.

In a regulatory report filed on July 24, 2026, TSMC detailed ownership changes among its senior executives for the month of June 2026, noting that Vice President Arthur Chuang reduced his holdings by 1 million shares while fellow vice presidents B.Z. Tien and Lipen Yuan each increased their stakes by 2,000 shares, including related-party holdings. The company also disclosed that there were no new share pledges, capital appropriations, unsecured bond issuances, or share cancellations during the period, while its treasury activity included NT$11.6 billion in fixed-income investments and NT$0.4 billion in dispositions, signaling incremental balance-sheet management rather than major strategic shifts.

These June 2026 disclosures provide investors with transparency into insider shareholding dynamics and TSMC’s conservative capital structure, as the absence of new bond issuance or share cancellations suggests the company continues to rely on internal resources and measured portfolio adjustments. For stakeholders, the modest insider trades and net increase in fixed-income holdings may indicate routine personal portfolio moves by executives and a cautious approach to liquidity and risk management amid ongoing global semiconductor demand.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSM) stock is a Hold
with a $440.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TSMC stock,
see the TSM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TSM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSM is a Outperform.

Overall score is driven primarily by very strong financial performance (high margins, improving growth, and conservative leverage) and an upbeat earnings outlook led by AI/HPC demand and raised full-year expectations. The main constraints are weak near-term technical momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD) and a somewhat demanding valuation with a low dividend yield, alongside guided near-term margin dilution from the N2 and overseas ramp.

To see Spark’s full report on TSM stock,
click here.

More about TSMC

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSMC) is a leading pure-play semiconductor foundry headquartered in Hsinchu, Taiwan, specializing in advanced integrated circuit manufacturing for global technology and electronics companies. The company fabricates chips for applications spanning consumer electronics, high-performance computing, automotive, and communications, and its shares are listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker TSM.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $1923B

Hercules Site Services Plc ( (GB:HERC) ) has shared an announcement.

Hercules plc disclosed that its chief executive officer, Brusk Korkmaz, has purchased 100,000 ordinary shares in the company at an average price of 39.75 pence per share via Hercules Real Estate Limited, an entity he controls. Following this transaction, Korkmaz’s beneficial holding has risen to 18,270,846 shares, representing 23.68% of the company’s issued share capital and signalling increased executive alignment with shareholders.

The share purchase, conducted on the London Stock Exchange’s AIM market, reinforces management’s visible commitment to the business at a time when investor focus on insider dealings and governance remains high. The enlarged stake may be viewed positively by stakeholders as an expression of confidence in Hercules’s prospects and could support market perception of the group’s strategic direction in the UK infrastructure and construction services sector.

Spark’s Take on HERC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HERC is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financial performance: strong revenue growth and improved free cash flow are positives, but inconsistent profitability and a still-levered balance sheet materially raise risk. Technicals are supportive due to strong recent momentum, though overbought indicators suggest elevated near-term volatility. Valuation is balanced by a high dividend yield but undermined by a negative P/E tied to weak earnings.

To see Spark’s full report on HERC stock,
click here.

More about Hercules Site Services Plc

Hercules plc is a leading UK infrastructure and construction services group, providing labour supply, civil engineering support and related services to major infrastructure and construction projects across the country. The company focuses on serving clients in transport, utilities and large-scale development sectors, leveraging its position on London’s AIM market to support growth.
